evidence.claim: Production-readiness manifest rolls up all checked QI CPU/GPU seed artifacts and explicitly keeps the gate bounded until production CPU/GPU ladders exist.
New five-seed bounded CPU QI ladder passes seeds 0..4 with zero process failures and maximum residual ratio below 1e-6.
Bounded QI CPU and one-GPU auto policy now converges at the higher scale-0.35 probe after host-sparse rescue.
Runner materializes deterministic QI neighboring decks with residual and acceptance gates.
CI-fast runner tests lock deterministic generation and gate semantics.
New scale-0.45 CPU QI probe passes at 11 x 23 x 45 x 4 in 106.0 s with public auto path and residual ratio below 5e-7.
Scale-0.50 CPU QI probe is checked in as timeout blocker evidence and is excluded from completion estimates.

Scale-0.50 QI solver matrix preserves compact failure breadcrumbs for auto, sparse_host_safe, sparse_lsmr, xblock_sparse_pc_gmres, initial-seed, post-minres, post-coarse, and LGMRES probes; all current routes fail, so the next step is a different global coupling strategy.
Scale-0.50 CPU QI seed now converges with promoted right-preconditioned x-block GMRES plus exact sparse LU block factors in about 12 s, residual ratio 4.16e-2.
Companion solver trace records the corrected explicit route: precondition_side=right, gmres_restart=80, 81 iterations, 85 matvecs, exact sparse_lu block factors.
Scale-0.50 one-GPU QI seed converges from a clean office clone in about 44.5 s, residual ratio 0.63, using the promoted right-preconditioned exact-xblock-LU policy.
GPU companion solver trace records precondition_side=right, gmres_restart=80, 69 iterations, 72 matvecs, exact sparse_lu block factors.

Scale-0.50 CPU five-seed QI ladder passes public solve_method=auto for seeds 0..4 with zero process failures, all outputs and solver traces written, max elapsed time 11.58 s, and max residual ratio 0.966.

Scale-0.50 one-GPU five-seed QI ladder passes public solve_method=auto for seeds 0..4 on office GPU 0 with zero process failures, all outputs and solver traces written, max elapsed time 41.18 s, and max residual ratio 0.963.

Scale-0.55 public-auto CPU next-scale probe timed out before output/trace even with a widened x-block sparse-PC cap, so QI production promotion remains blocked by larger-scale sparse factorization cost.
Scale-0.55 CPU QI successor converges with the widened profiling window after raising the full-FP host x-block exact-LU cap to 30000; active size 52637, elapsed 21.5 s, residual ratio 8.25e-3.

Scale-0.55 CPU hard seed 3 now follows the public auto policy to left-preconditioned x-block GMRES at active size 52637, avoiding the right-PC slow mode and converging in about 47 s with residual ratio 2.98e-3.

Scale-0.55 CPU five-seed QI ladder passes public auto for seeds 0..4 with left-preconditioned x-block GMRES, zero failures/timeouts, all outputs and traces, max elapsed 44.5 s, and max residual ratio 5.88e-3.
Scale-0.55 one-GPU five-seed QI ladder passes from a clean office checkout with zero failures/timeouts, all outputs and traces, max elapsed 206.7 s, and max residual ratio 8.28e-3.
Scale-0.60 CPU seed-0 QI probe passes at active size 81377 with left-preconditioned exact-xblock-LU GMRES in 42.2 s and residual ratio 3.42e-3.
Scale-0.60 one-GPU seed-0 QI probe passes at active size 81377 from a clean office checkout in 145.1 s and residual ratio 4.68e-3.
Scale-0.60 CPU five-seed QI ladder passes accepted-converged strict gates with the bounded side-probe/LGMRES rescue; max elapsed 307.9 s and max residual ratio 8.42e-3.
Scale-0.60 GPU seed3 LGMRES-rescue probe times out at 620 s on office, showing the CPU rescue is not a GPU default.
Scale-0.60 GPU seed3 right-preconditioned GMRES side-switch probe also times out at 620 s, keeping the hard-seed GPU lane open.
Scale-0.60 GPU hard-seed rejected-probe summary records failed two-level x-block, GCROT(m,k), BiCGStab, post-correction-only, and JAX-factor attempts as blocker evidence.

Scale-0.60 global-coupling/operator-reuse rejected-probe summary records opt-in smoothed global coupling, assembled-operator reuse, keep-left side probing, and JAX-factor exposure as implemented but not default-promoted.
Large-QI explicit device-Krylov requests now have a metadata-visible non-autodiff host fallback artifact that preserves the measured side-probe seed plus LGMRES rescue.

Reusable JAX-compatible Galerkin QI coarse preconditioner infrastructure eliminates synthetic residuals to roundoff with DeviceCSR/JIT coverage, but remains to be wired into the production GPU hard-seed loop.
Focused QI coarse tests cover regularized JAX normal-equation solves, Galerkin correction reuse, and JIT/device CSR application without requiring a large solve.

The opt-in production Galerkin QI preconditioner is now fail-closed: additive/multiplicative/damping probe candidates are selected only when they reduce the true residual, otherwise the base preconditioner remains active.
Focused policy tests cover Galerkin mode parsing, damping parsing, residual-reduction selection, nonfinite rejection, and required-drop gates.
Standalone device-local QI smoother infrastructure now provides a pure-JAX CSR damped-Jacobi/stationary smoother with fail-closed diagonal validation and JIT-safe DeviceCSR matvec reuse.

Focused QI smoother tests cover CSR diagonal extraction, missing-diagonal rejection, permissive partial diagnostics, JIT stationary sweeps, and compatibility with the existing two-level true-residual probe.

Device-local QI smoother now has an opt-in residual-minimizing step policy and fail-closed true-residual seed probe; the bounded synthetic gate reduces residual norm from 1.0 to 0.8944 and rejects stricter non-material improvements.
Focused QI smoother tests cover the residual-minimizing accepted seed, fail-closed strict-improvement rejection, and JSON-safe probe metadata.
Scale-0.60 public-auto one-GPU QI hard seed timed out at 600 s on office after the xmg/strong-fallback route reached residual 2.262e-6 against target 3.021e-13, with no output or solver trace.

Scale-0.60 forced x-block one-GPU QI hard seed exercised the fail-closed Galerkin production hook; rank-32 Galerkin was rejected because the probe worsened the residual, then right-preconditioned GMRES timed out after 1300 matvecs.

Scale-0.60 forced x-block one-GPU LGMRES rescue selected the intended method-rescue path but timed out after 950 matvecs, so GPU promotion requires a stronger residual-reducing algorithm rather than another solver-label toggle.

Bounded 13 x 13 x 15 x 4 QI nfp=2 office-GPU operator-reuse rerun activates the matrix-free route, skips local x-block factors, writes fail-safe trace metadata, and records corrected device-cycle accounting, but fails the residual gate and is not production true-device-QI evidence.

gates: Residual ratio must remain below the requested gate on CPU and GPU.
Production-resolution public-auto promotion still requires wider seeds and wall-time/memory budgets.
No QI-specific default branch may bypass the public auto solver policy.
The exact-xblock-LU cap and device-host fallback must remain scoped to non-differentiable full-FP host x-block factors.
True device-QI remains closed deferred and cannot block release until a new residual-reducing GPU hard-seed artifact passes.
next_actions: Keep the production non-autodiff host fallback release-ready by preserving metadata that records fallback use, non-autodiff scope, output/trace writes, and residual convergence.

Do not widen the public full-FP 3D x-block sparse-PC default window from the new GPU artifacts; public-auto, forced x-block GMRES, and forced x-block LGMRES scale-0.60 hard-seed reruns timed out under strict gates.

Revisit true device-QI only with a stronger residual-reducing algorithm; the checked mid-size operator-reuse route now proves factor-skip/accounting/trace infrastructure, but the residual remains far above target and the scale-0.60 GPU hard-seed artifact is still the promotion blocker.
lanes.second_push_result: scale-0.50 and scale-0.55 public auto CPU and one-GPU five-seed QI ladders pass; scale-0.60 CPU now also passes seeds 0..4 with the bounded side-probe/LGMRES rescue. The large-QI production escape hatch is closed by a release-ready non-autodiff host fallback for explicit device-Krylov requests. True differentiable/device-QI is closed deferred post-release: the JAX Galerkin coarse preconditioner is implemented and fail-closed, and the new residual-minimizing device smoother improves a bounded local probe, but the scale-0.60 GPU hard seed still lacks a residual-clean accepted device-local artifact.
lanes.deferred_reason: The current release can ship the documented non-autodiff large-QI host fallback, but true differentiable/device-QI and production-resolution CPU/GPU QI ladders remain post-release research until GPU hard-seed and production ladder artifacts pass.
lanes.title: Production-resolution QI CPU/GPU robustness

evidence.claim: Production-floor PAS-TZ tzfft CPU artifact converges at the collaborator floor without dense allocation.
No-op PAS matrix-free corrections now avoid the extra candidate matvec while preserving residual history and reject reason.
Benchmark harness now rejects default-promotion candidates that regress elapsed time or peak RSS against baseline.
Docs now distinguish promoted PAS memory routes from rejected experimental routes.
Structured PAS-TZ guard metadata records production-grid memory rejection estimates without allocating dense guards.
Matrix-free PAS tests cover tiny-update and candidate-size fail-fast gates that avoid wasted candidate matvecs.
PAS-TZ fallback benchmark rows now require guarded fallback provenance before any candidate can pass all gates or be listed as promotion eligible.
Regression tests lock guarded PAS-TZ provenance requirements for benchmark promotion summaries.
Production-floor PAS-TZ tzfft GPU artifact converges at the collaborator floor with residual matching the CPU floor artifact.
Release checker rejects malformed or policy-invalid PAS benchmark artifacts before they can support runtime/memory claims.
Matrix-free PAS correction now has a fail-fast live-byte preflight that rejects oversized candidates before update allocation or candidate residual matvecs.
PAS matrix-free benchmark dry-runs can record a maximum candidate byte budget so unsafe production-floor probes fail before allocating the candidate path.
Opt-in PAS production real-solve probes now require --max-candidate-bytes by default, with explicit opt-out provenance for unbudgeted diagnostics.
Checked byte-budget dry-run records geometry4 and HSX as byte-safe launchable short probes while holding geometry11 for missing promotion-facing artifact evidence.

Geometry4 production real-solve probe is residual-clean and solver-path stable, but both tzfft candidates regress runtime/RSS against the checked baseline and therefore fail the default-promotion gate.
HSX production real-solve probe is residual-clean and solver-path stable, but tzfft has a runtime regression and tzfft_lgmres has a memory regression, so neither is promoted.
PAS matrix-free correction now has an opt-in streamed update path that builds candidates from bounded residual chunks without materializing a full dense update vector.

PAS benchmark gates now reject guarded-correction variants unless metadata proves streamed/no-full-update behavior, and focused tests cover chunk equivalence, byte-budget rejection, and residual-gate semantics.
Bounded streamed-update artifact records geometry4, HSX, and geometry11 metadata probes with stream_update_chunks=true, full_update_materialized=false, 8 ok rows, and all_expected_gates_met=true.
Production PAS-TZ guarded-correction metadata now records the blocker that minres still requires full residual/full-direction work arrays, preventing an unsafe streamed-correction promotion.

gates: Keep only routes that improve runtime or memory while preserving true residual and Fortran parity.
Reject fallback candidates that avoid allocation but do not reduce the residual.
CPU and GPU default policy changes require bounded artifacts before README-facing claims.
next_actions: Keep the current guarded PAS-TZ defaults; the new geometry4 and HSX real-solve probes are residual-clean but do not improve runtime/RSS.

Implement a real chunked PAS-TZ guarded-correction callback before the next geometry4/HSX/geometry11 CPU/GPU artifact campaign; the current bounded streamed update path proves metadata-case chunking but production minres still materializes full vectors.
Promote a PAS route only after a residual-clean CPU/GPU artifact improves runtime or peak memory on geometry4/HSX/geometry11 floors.
lanes.second_push_result: CPU and GPU production-floor PAS-TZ tzfft artifacts now converge at 25 x 51 x 100 x 4 with bounded memory; matrix-free PAS candidates now have element and byte preflights plus an opt-in streamed update path, real-solve probes require explicit byte budgets by default, the bounded streamed-update artifact proves no-full-update metadata-case chunking for geometry4/HSX/geometry11, and default promotion remains evidence-gated until production PAS-TZ guarded correction is chunked and produces a residual-clean runtime/RSS win
lanes.title: PAS and geometry-rich runtime/memory offenders

evidence.claim: Transport-worker benchmark now has a plan-only mode that records deterministic RHS partitioning, skipped worker counts, timing semantics, and release-gate semantics without launching solves.
Single-case sharded-solve benchmark now has a plan-only mode that records timeout, backend, sharding, and non-release scaling semantics before launching expensive samples.
Multi-GPU case-throughput benchmark now has a plan-only mode that records warmup, sequential one-GPU, and parallel two-GPU child process plans before launching GPU work.
Sharded matvec benchmark now records deterministic plan-only metadata for device counts, padding, compiled-hot-loop timing, and memory-gate semantics.
New tests validate capped worker plans, cold-start rejection semantics, and GPU worker schedule coalescing.
New tests keep multi-GPU throughput plans non-release and verify the internal sharded-solve command contract.
New tests validate single-case sharded plan JSON and ensure it remains an experimental non-release claim.
Release-facing GPU transport-worker throughput figure is checked in.
Parallelism docs describe process, worker, GPU, and validation routes.
Runtime policy module owns worker-count and backend decisions.
Pure multi-GPU case-throughput audit rejects cold/mixed timing, non-GPU payloads, release overclaims, and sub-unity speedups.
Fresh office two-GPU case-throughput artifact completed under timeout guards but failed the speedup gate at 0.337x, so it is blocker evidence rather than a release claim.
Transport-worker benchmark plans and measured payloads now record compile-amortization metadata before release scaling claims can pass.
Pure scaling audit fails explicit release claims that omit compile-amortization evidence and preserves explicit non-release status.
Release-facing GPU transport-worker artifact records a measured two-worker speedup above one worker for independent transport tasks.
Office two-GPU case-throughput artifact is checked as blocker evidence and explicitly fails the speedup gate instead of becoming a release claim.
Pure parallel claim-scope audit now separates independent whichRHS throughput, independent case throughput, and experimental single-case sharded solves before speedup claims are inspected.
Transport and sharded benchmark plans now write parallel_claim_scope metadata so single-case sharding cannot be mistaken for release-grade strong scaling.

Single-case sharded solve and matvec plans now record compiled operator-reuse gates, persistent compile-cache metadata, warm-run amortization semantics, and fail-closed deterministic residual/output gates before scaling claims can pass.
Parallel scaling audits now fail CI-gate when single-case sharded solve artifacts omit compiled operator-reuse metadata or deterministic residual/output gate schema.

Single-case sharded solve benchmark can now run a deterministic 1-vs-N output probe that writes solution-vector digests, relative L2 difference, evidence source, and vector paths before any scaling promotion.

Dry-run 1-vs-2 GPU sharded-solve artifact records operator_reuse_gate.passes=true, deterministic_output_gate.passes=false with plan_only_probe_requested evidence, and release_scaling_supported=false because office was dirty/outdated.

Clean-office 1-vs-2 GPU sharded-solve fail-closed artifact records a stable 1-GPU hot solve (4.047 s) and a 2-GPU timeout at the 300 s cap; deterministic output was skipped after timing failure, so this is blocker evidence rather than a scaling claim.
Companion figure visualizes the clean-office fail-closed single-case 1-vs-2 GPU sharded solve attempt.

gates: Case-parallel and transport-worker paths must preserve serial output ordering and parity.
Strong-scaling claims require per-worker work large enough to amortize setup.
Single-case multi-GPU remains experimental unless a measured publication-grade artifact improves wall time.
next_actions: Do not promote single-case multi-GPU strong scaling from the current sharded solve path: clean-office measured evidence times out on 2 GPUs even though 1 GPU completes the hot solve in about 4 s.

Next algorithmic step is a true compiled device operator/coarse-reuse path that keeps the state sharded across Krylov iterations and avoids per-child setup/host-collective overhead; rerun the same deterministic-output artifact only after that implementation changes.
Keep release-facing parallel claims scoped to independent transport-worker/case throughput until the 1-vs-2 single-case artifact both beats 1 GPU and passes deterministic output parity.
lanes.second_push_result: release-facing transport-worker parallelism is now evidence-ready with audited GPU throughput artifacts, claim-scope metadata, strict non-release classification for setup-dominated two-GPU/single-case experiments, fail-closed compiled operator-reuse gates, and a deterministic-output probe path that produces digest/residual evidence before future sharded-solve claims Clean-office follow-up on 2026-05-17 converted the remaining 1-vs-2 GPU sharded-solve item from dry-run uncertainty into measured fail-closed blocker evidence: 1 GPU completes the hot solve in 4.047 s, while the 2-GPU child times out at the 300 s cap and skips deterministic-output probing.
lanes.title: Executable parallelism and strong-scaling evidence

evidence.claim: Dynamic docstring tests now cover public APIs in split policy modules so refactors stay discoverable.
New pure-policy tests improve coverage of transport solver, backend, and parallel environment decisions without launching slow solves.
Source-map docs identify policy, solver, transport, geometry, and validation modules.
Release-claim metadata is enforced without launching expensive solves.
Host refinement logic was split out of the driver and became directly testable.
Fast tests cover measured candidate promotion, memory metric ordering, failed-baseline rescue, and tie-breaking.
Residual-gate tests cover env parsing, negative threshold normalization, nonfinite diagnostics, and array failure collection.
Fast policy tests now cover dense accelerator guards, sparse-direct eligibility, host-GMRES rejection paths, and sparse factor dtype overrides.
Release metadata tests cover malformed manifests, invalid status/docs, and non-list manifest failures.
Bounded 3D full-FP x-block sparse-PC auto policy is now isolated in a pure helper with direct tests instead of hidden driver branching.
Focused policy tests cover the QI x-block sparse-PC auto window and its backend, size, species, Phi1/PAS, and differentiability guards.
Artifact tests ensure CPU/GPU QI public-auto evidence stays checked and solver traces remain present.
Release-gate checker validates status, kind, non-empty literature/claim/source/test/gate lists, and source/test/script/artifact path existence for release-ready and closed-deferred lanes.
Deferred-lane gate tests prove closed-deferred validation lanes can remain nonblocking while still requiring valid source, test, script, and artifact paths.
Fast CLI, plotting, validation-figure, and benchmark-artifact tests cover I/O/error branches without launching expensive solves.
QI Galerkin residual-selection policy is split out of the driver with direct fast tests, reducing the remaining untested hard-seed branch surface without adding slow solves.
RHSMode=1 handoff residual-improvement logic is split into a source-mapped helper with direct finite/nonfinite boundary tests, reducing driver acceptance-branch surface without launching solves.
CI coverage collection now runs in three parallel shards and combines artifacts in a report job, preserving the fail-under gate without a single long-running coverage runner.

Lightweight profiling helpers now have module/class/factory docstrings and direct tests for environment opt-ins, RSS fallback units, unavailable OS/JAX memory sampling, and profiler formatting without launching solves.

gates: Coverage gains must come from focused module tests, not slow full-solve duplication.
Large driver splits must preserve CLI/API parity and existing solver provenance.
CI-fast checks must remain within the documented time budget.
next_actions: Continue extracting pure solver/path/diagnostic helpers from v3_driver.py with fast focused tests before raising the package-wide fail-under threshold; profiling.py is now fully exercised by no-solve helper tests.
Keep the sharded CI coverage gate green and use future coverage reports to choose real low-cost invariants, not slow full-solve padding.
lanes.second_push_result: additional policy extraction, RHSMode=1 handoff refactoring, profiling-helper coverage, source-map documentation, public-auto QI dispatch tests, sharded CI coverage collection, CI-fast release gates, deferred-lane path hygiene, and fast CLI/validation I/O coverage make the refactor/CI lane target-saturated for this release; raising package-wide coverage still requires more driver decomposition rather than slow solve padding
lanes.title: Refactor, source map, coverage, and CI readiness

evidence.claim: New docs page states the skip-safe workflow, commands, differentiability contract, and non-claims.
New status scaffold reports optional backend availability and exact proxy-gradient commands without importing optional packages.
Optional differentiable geometry handoff example exists and is documented.
Finite-beta VMEC-to-SFINCS profile figure is checked in for user-facing docs.
Adapter module isolates JAX-native geometry inputs from file-backed VMEC loading.
Optional workflow tests cover backend readiness and measured adoption summaries without hard dependencies.
Shared pure-JAX Boozer-spectrum proxy transport objective checks full spectral finite-difference gradients and JVP/dot-product consistency without optional dependencies.
Optional workflow tests exercise the shared proxy transport gradient gate in default CI.
VMEC/Boozer workflow status and pipeline summaries now include a no-solve provenance gate that records proxy-vs-kinetic scope and confirms no kinetic solve was executed.
Workflow tests require the no-solve provenance gate in backend status and file-backed summary payloads.
VMEC/Boozer adapters now expose a machine-readable kinetic transport scalar contract that separates geometry preparation, kinetic operator assembly, solve, reduction, and gradient validation stages.
Autodiff pipeline and status examples emit the kinetic scalar contract so public examples cannot overclaim full VMEC-boundary-to-kinetic gradients before the solve stage is wired.

The no-solve provenance gate is now shared in jax_geometry_adapters and used by both the file-backed autodiff pipeline and skip-safe workflow status example, with tests for complete provenance, missing provenance, and scalar-contract tampering.

gates: End-to-end optimization claims require differentiating through a validated geometry-to-transport scalar.
Boozer and VMEC optional dependencies must fail gracefully when not installed.
Profile-current and ambipolar outputs must pass convergence gates before publication claims.
next_actions: Wire a validated VMEC/Boozer geometry-to-kinetic-transport scalar before claiming full VMEC-boundary-to-SFINCS gradients.
Keep VMEC/Boozer optional dependencies skip-safe in default CI and keep no-solve provenance explicit in public examples.
lanes.second_push_result: scoped geometry-proxy workflow now has shared proxy transport-gradient gates, a centralized machine-readable no-solve provenance gate, tests for file provenance and scalar-contract tampering, and an explicit VMEC/Boozer-to-kinetic scalar contract; full kinetic transport gradients remain out of scope until the kinetic solve stage is validated
lanes.title: vmec_jax to booz_xform_jax to sfincs_jax differentiable workflow

evidence.claim: New tests run measured optional Lineax, Equinox, and JAXopt gates while skipping safely if optional packages are absent.
Docs record the memory/runtime rationale for short-recurrence Krylov, checkpointing, and JAX profiling.
Performance-technique docs record keep/reject gates for solver candidates.
Solver-selection policy is isolated enough to host future lineax/equinox experiments behind gates.
Lineax benchmark now writes adoption summaries that separate synthetic speedups from real SFINCS solver-error statuses.
Equinox/JAXopt gate now reports measured directional-gradient and optimization summary decisions.
Lineax adoption summaries now fail closed when real SFINCS rows are residual-clean but return non-success Lineax statuses, preventing residual-only promotion.

gates: No new JAX ecosystem dependency is promoted unless it reduces runtime or memory on a documented case.
Any replacement solver must preserve autodiff semantics or explicitly remain in the non-differentiable CLI lane.
Hard-coded branch thresholds should move to measured policy gates when evidence exists.
next_actions: Resolve Lineax real-SFINCS non-success statuses before any solver promotion; residual-clean status mismatches are now explicit blockers.
Keep Equinox/JAXopt examples as optional bounded optimization workflows rather than hard dependencies.
lanes.second_push_result: optional Lineax, Equinox, and JAXopt gates are target-saturated for release: useful workflows are documented, real-SFINCS Lineax residual-clean status mismatches remain unpromoted, and no hard dependency is added
lanes.title: JAX ecosystem solver/preconditioner evaluation

evidence.claim: New CI-fast research-lane checker prevents subjective or unsupported completion claims.
New tests enforce evidence paths, non-regressing completion percentages, and substantial per-lane deltas.
README-facing Fortran/JAX runtime and active-memory comparison figure is checked in.
Validation manifest records release-ready, bounded-proxy, and deferred lanes explicitly.
Benchmark artifact policy prevents stale or inconsistent runtime/memory claims.
New doc-claim tests prevent stale benchmark ratio, figure, and scope wording from shipping.
Benchmark-summary tests check the checked-in report against default regeneration output.
README benchmark-doc tests now require the 39-case suite counts, filtered runtime-table row count, and excluded low-reference-runtime cases to match the canonical summary JSON.

gates: README table and figure must be generated from the same canonical filtered rows.
No jax_error or max_attempts row may appear in public release-facing parity claims.
Low-resolution CI smoke rows must not be promoted into runtime comparison claims.
next_actions: Regenerate final plots only after accepted performance-policy changes alter benchmark data.
Keep README/docs scope language tied to canonical checked benchmark rows.
lanes.second_push_result: target saturated for docs/release consistency gates; README suite counts, filtered runtime-table scope, excluded low-reference-runtime cases, ratios, figures, and benchmark JSON regeneration checks are now tied to the canonical summary without regenerating benchmark data in this push
lanes.title: Benchmark, parity, README, and documentation publication surface

purpose: Machine-readable completion tracker for the coordinated large-push research/performance lanes. Percentages are engineering readiness estimates backed by checked-in evidence, not physics claims by themselves.
second_push_assessment: large push is now at 95.9% overall: production large-QI has a release-ready non-autodiff host fallback plus a bounded residual-minimizing device smoother, true device-QI is still deferred until scale-0.60 GPU hard-seed artifacts accept a device-local path, PAS has streamed metadata-case update probes and explicit production minres full-vector blocker diagnostics, scaling has deterministic-output digest probes plus release-promotion blockers for single-case sharding, refactor/CI has source-mapped profiling helper coverage, VMEC/Boozer examples share a no-solve scalar-contract gate, Lineax fails closed on residual-clean non-success statuses, and README benchmark-table scope is locked to canonical summary JSON. Remaining open work is now evidence-defined: implement a real chunked PAS-TZ guarded correction, run a clean-office deterministic 1-vs-2 GPU artifact, continue driver decomposition before raising package-wide coverage thresholds, and revisit true device-QI only with a residual-reducing hard-seed algorithm rather than another solver-label toggle.
